title: Bluff: Food & Drink
description: One answer is fake — can you spot the bluff?

questions.question: Which country produces the most coffee in the world?
questions.explanation: Brazil produces about one-third of the world's coffee supply.

questions.question: What is the most consumed beverage in the world after water?
questions.explanation: Tea is the second most consumed beverage globally after water.

questions.question: Which fruit has its seeds on the outside?
questions.explanation: Strawberries have about 200 seeds on their outer surface.

questions.question: What is the most expensive spice in the world by weight?
questions.explanation: Saffron can cost $5,000–$10,000 per kilogram — it takes 150,000 flowers for 1 kg.

questions.question: Which cheese is the most consumed in the world?
questions.explanation: Mozzarella is the world's most consumed cheese, largely due to pizza.

questions.question: What is the hottest chili pepper in the world?
questions.explanation: The Carolina Reaper averages 1.6 million Scoville Heat Units.

questions.question: Which nut is used to make marzipan?
questions.explanation: Marzipan is made from ground almonds and sugar.

questions.question: Which country invented pizza?
questions.explanation: Modern pizza originated in Naples, Italy in the 18th century.

questions.question: What is the main ingredient in guacamole?
questions.explanation: Guacamole is made primarily from mashed avocado.

questions.question: Which country consumes the most chocolate per capita?
questions.explanation: Switzerland consumes about 10 kg of chocolate per person per year.

questions.question: What grain is used to make traditional Japanese sake?
questions.explanation: Sake is brewed from polished rice using a special fermentation process.

questions.question: Which vegetable was the first to be grown in space?
questions.explanation: Potatoes were the first vegetable grown in space aboard the Space Shuttle Columbia in 1995.

questions.question: What is the most eaten food in the world?
questions.explanation: Rice is the staple food for more than half the world's population.

questions.question: Which country is the largest producer of olive oil?
questions.explanation: Spain produces about 45% of the world's olive oil.

questions.question: What is wasabi traditionally made from?
questions.explanation: Real wasabi comes from the Wasabia japonica plant stem, though most restaurants use dyed horseradish.
